summary: Iteration 10 verified: (1) /api/health/time, (2) /api/games/{id}/leaderboard, (3) Dashboard MY ACTIVE TOURNAMENTS table refactor, (4) GameLeaderboard page reached via game-card click. Backend pytest 5/5 PASS (1 sort-order test SKIPPED because no game has >=2 leaderboard rows in this DB). Frontend Playwright PASS for: login as davidjovanic@yahoo.com.au, dashboard shows empty no-tournaments testid (user has 0 active tournaments) with TopNav visible, /games shows 101 cards with TopNav, clicking first card navigates to /games/{id}/leaderboard, GameLeaderboard renders back-to-games link, heading '<game> — LEADERBOARD', empty state no-game-results testid (no completed matches), TopNav present, and back link returns to /games.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py L3437 wraps ObjectId(game_id) in try/except → returns 404 for both malformed and non-existent ids. Verified both cases.
server.py L3450 only counts tournaments with status=='completed' — open tournaments and currently-in-progress matches are correctly excluded. Good.

server.py L3460 net_credits for tournament winner is `stake * (n_players - 1)` (gross of own stake). This matches platform-payout model where winner takes everyone else's stake; loser net is -stake. Consistent.
server.py L3468 h2h winnings credit `stake_amount` to winner — this is the *opponent* stake (correct), but ensure m['stake_amount'] is per-player and not pot; this matches code elsewhere.
server.py L3494 sort key is (wins, net_credits) descending — correct tie-breaker behaviour.

Dashboard.js L203 my-tournaments-table renders the same 6 columns specified in the requirements (Opponent / Game / Platform / Date & Time / Stake / Status). The opponentLabel ternary correctly handles 1v1 vs >2-player tournaments. Status mapping covers open/in_progress/pending_confirmation/disputed; falls back to upper-cased raw status for any unknown state (defensive). Solid.
GameLeaderboard.js L19 uses withCredentials — matches httpOnly cookie auth pattern. On error it toasts + navigates back to /games which prevents stuck states.

GameLeaderboard.js L65 `isMe` check highlights current user row with bg-[#FF3B30]/10 + (YOU) badge — verified the className conditional but couldn't visually confirm without a matching row in DB. Code is correct.

BrowseGames.js L221 navigates on game-card click — note: this also fires from the entire card area including the cover image; there is no separate 'view leaderboard' affordance, which could confuse users who expect a card click to open the game profile. Cosmetic UX concern.
GameLeaderboard.js L9 builds API base from process.env.REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL — correct.

success_rate.backend: 100% (5/5 ran, 1 skipped for lack of seed data with >=2 rows)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (login → dashboard empty-state → /games with 101 cards → click card → GameLeaderboard → back). TopNav verified on Dashboard, BrowseGames, and GameLeaderboard.

test_credentials: Regular user davidjovanic@yahoo.com.au / Andmay123 (provided). Wallet 1000 CR, no active tournaments, no completed matches.
seed_data_creation: None. Used existing davidjovanic user and existing game catalog (101 games).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend cookie-auth via /api/auth/login (no token in body); use requests.Session(). Iter10 features all pass. The leaderboard 'row population + current-user highlight' visual case was not exercised because davidjovanic has zero completed matches and no game in the DB has >=2 rows; to test it, complete a tournament or confirm a competition_match first (see /api/competitions and /api/tournaments flows). 404 on /api/games/{id}/leaderboard fires for both malformed ids and valid-but-missing 24-hex ids. Health endpoint is unauthenticated and returns ISO-8601 strings + tz_offset_hours (float).
